Santiago Bragagnolo

Home > Santiago Bragagnolo
Biography｜Martial Training｜Academic Career｜Philosophy

Biography

Born in 1982 in Argentina, Santiago Bragagnolo (43) is a martial arts instructor whose journey reflects an unwavering dedication spanning over three decades. Of Argentine-Italian nationality and resident in France since 2012, he embodies the synthesis between Eastern martial tradition and Western excellence.

Martial Training

His journey began at age 11, embarking on a path that would lead him to master multiple disciplines: Judo, Jiujitsu, Taekwondo, the Filipino school of Eskrima Doce Pares, and Kung Fu, where he achieved Second Duan.

Since 2014, Santiago has dedicated himself to studying Shaolin Kung Fu in France. His pursuit of excellence connected him with Grandmaster Shi Miaohai, warrior monk of the 36th Shaolin generation, with whom he had the privilege to participate in intensive seminars in 2015, 2016 and 2018.

From 2017, he began an exceptional formative relationship with the Wutan School of Taipei, travelling regularly to Taiwan to receive direct teaching from Chairman Grandmaster Lin Zhongxi. These private immersions (2017, 2019, 2022, 2023, 2024, with trips scheduled for 2025-2026) have conferred upon him a profound understanding of traditional Wutan styles, certifying his authority to transmit this ancestral lineage in Europe.

Academic Career

Concurrently, Santiago has built a distinguished career as a software engineer, with a Master's in Engineering and Projects and a PhD in Software Modernisation. This academic formation reflects the same discipline he applies in the dojang.

Philosophy

A first-time father, he balances his role as martial instructor with his family life in Lille, where he transmits not only techniques, but a philosophy of life that integrates body, mind and spirit.
